Installation

Choose the installation method that works best for you.

Security Notice: Be Paranoid

While the author says these scripts are safe, thou shalt be paranoid. We encourage you to:

  1. Read the installation scripts before running them
  2. Check the release checksums linked below
  3. Verify checksums if you’re extra cautious
  4. Build from source if you trust no one (we respect that)

The scripts are open source and designed to be auditable. Your security paranoia is valid and encouraged!

Quick Install (Recommended)

macOS / Linux

Or download and review first (recommended for the security-conscious):

View install.sh source | Releases (see checksums)

Windows (PowerShell)

Or download and review first:

View install.ps1 source | Releases (see checksums)

Manual Download

If you prefer to download binaries directly without running scripts.

macOS

Linux

Windows

Download the ZIP archive from the releases page and extract it.

PowerShell (manual):

FreeBSD

Container (Docker/Podman)

Multi-arch container images are available for linux/amd64 and linux/arm64:

Available Tags

TagDescription
latestLatest release
vX.Y.ZSpecific version (e.g., v0.0.25)
X.Y.ZVersion without prefix (e.g., 0.0.25)

Build from Source

For development or to get the latest unreleased features. Also the most secure option if you’re truly paranoid (we appreciate you).

Prerequisites

  • Go 1.21+
  • just command runner (optional)

Clone and Build

Go Install

If you have Go 1.21+:

Make sure ~/go/bin is in your PATH:

Available Archives

PlatformArchitectureArchive
Linuxx64versionator-linux-amd64.tar.gz
Linuxarm64versionator-linux-arm64.tar.gz
macOSIntelversionator-darwin-amd64.tar.gz
macOSApple Siliconversionator-darwin-arm64.tar.gz
Windowsx64versionator-windows-amd64.exe.zip
Windowsarm64versionator-windows-arm64.exe.zip
FreeBSDx64versionator-freebsd-amd64.tar.gz

All binaries are statically compiled with no dependencies.

Security Verification

Each release includes SHA256 checksums. Verify archive integrity:

Verify Installation

Expected output:

Updating

Using Install Scripts

Just run the install script again:

macOS/Linux:

Windows:

Go Install

Troubleshooting

Command not found

Ensure the installation directory is in your PATH:

Permission denied

Use sudo when installing to system directories, or install to a user directory:

macOS: “Cannot be opened” or “Unverified developer”

macOS Gatekeeper blocks unsigned binaries. Solutions:

1. Use the install script (Recommended) - automatically removes quarantine:

2. Remove quarantine manually:

3. Allow in System Settings:

  • Try to run versionator - macOS will block it
  • Open System Settings → Privacy & Security
  • Click “Open Anyway”

4. Build from source - avoids Gatekeeper entirely.

Next Steps

  1. Quick Start - Get up and running
  2. Shell Completion - Set up tab completion
